overall_assessment: Not delivery-ready in round 01. Focused unit and feature evidence covered part of the cutover, but runtime/integration evidence and occurrence-specific backend assertions were incomplete.
recommended_path: Repair stale integration fixtures, add multi-occurrence invite and share-code occurrence assertions, and keep ADB/device evidence explicit as a deferred consolidated gate if not run in this round.

findings.title: Runtime and integration evidence for the reopened QA symptoms was deferred
findings.rationale: Round 01 listed exact ADB/device symptoms as deferred and one modified integration fixture asserted parent event id instead of selected occurrence id.
findings.suggested_action: Repair the fixture to include a selected occurrence and keep the ADB scenarios as explicit consolidated device-gate evidence until they can be run.

findings.title: Occurrence-scoped duplicate and credited-acceptance behavior was not tested across two occurrences of the same event
findings.rationale: The invite tests used helper target refs that always selected the first occurrence, so event-keyed duplicate or credited-winner bugs could still pass.
findings.suggested_action: Add tests with one event and two occurrences proving duplicates and supersession are scoped to occurrence, not parent event.

findings.title: Share materialization/acceptance and visible received-invite occurrence context were weakly asserted
findings.rationale: Round 01 did not consistently assert share materialized/accepted response and persisted edge occurrence identity, leaving the share-code root cause under-protected.
findings.suggested_action: Assert target_ref.occurrence_id and persisted InviteEdge.occurrence_id on share create, materialize, accept, and replay paths; add Flutter visible occurrence coverage or document the remaining ADB gate.
